description: Correct context/rules/memory.md's false 'log.md tracked inside' daily-log persistence claim to match .gitignore reality, and guard it with a read-only tier-A eval probe.

userStories.title: Correct the false 'tracked inside' claim in memory.md
userStories.description: As an agent reading context/rules/memory.md, I want the Layout block to accurately describe daily-log persistence so I am not misled about which memory survives a fresh clone.
acceptanceCriteria: context/rules/memory.md no longer contains the string 'tracked inside' (grep -c 'tracked inside' context/rules/memory.md returns 0)
The Layout code-block line for log.md accurately states the directory is gitignored and the daily log is local-only (e.g. '# daily append log (gitignored - local-only; not committed)')

Prose inside or directly below the Layout fenced code block states: the memory/YYYY-MM-DD/ directory is gitignored via .gitignore 'memory/[0-9]*/'; daily logs are a local-only working journal; only MEMORY.md, README.md, and topic notes persist in git
That same note records that the two pre-ignore logs (memory/2026-05-03, memory/2026-05-04) remain tracked as historical vestige
No other file under context/ or .claude/skills/ claims daily logs are git-tracked (grep -rniE 'log\.md.*track|track.*log\.md|tracked inside' context/ .claude/skills/ returns 0 matches)
The Layout fenced code block still renders cleanly (opening and closing triple-backtick fences intact)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Iter 1 (2026-06-13): removed 'tracked inside' from Layout block, added gitignore/local-only/vestige note; grep ACs + typecheck/lint/test:scripts green. Commit b9e8b47.

userStories.title: Add a tier-A probe guarding the corrected claim
userStories.description: As the harness, I want a deterministic probe that fails if the false claim returns or the gitignore rule is removed, so this lesson is retained the way other lessons are.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/probes/memory-gitignore-claim.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
It mirrors evals/probes/boot-lint-glob.sh: '#!/usr/bin/env bash', then header comments '# tier: A', '# source: issue #101', '# desc: <one line>', then 'set -euo pipefail'
Repo root is resolved relative to BASH_SOURCE (not pwd), matching boot-lint-glob.sh
3-state oracle: exit 0 PASS, exit 1 REGRESSION, exit 2 SKIPPED
SKIPPED (exit 2) if EITHER .gitignore OR context/rules/memory.md is absent, printing 'SKIPPED: <which file>' to stderr (guards the grep-on-missing-file false-PASS for Assertion B)
Assertion A: an UNCOMMENTED 'memory/[0-9]*/' line exists in .gitignore (skip comment lines, e.g. grep -v '^[[:space:]]*#' .gitignore | grep -qF 'memory/[0-9]*/'); REGRESSION if missing
Assertion B: context/rules/memory.md does NOT contain the literal 'tracked inside'; REGRESSION if present
On the corrected tree, bash evals/probes/memory-gitignore-claim.sh exits 0 and prints a 'PASS: ...' line
Negative test confirmed: reintroducing 'tracked inside' makes it exit 1, and removing the .gitignore line makes it exit 1; both temporary edits reverted afterward

bash -n passes and shellcheck -S warning evals/probes/memory-gitignore-claim.sh is clean (run manually; the CI boot-lint glob is NOT expanded to evals/probes/ because that would flip the boot-lint-glob probe)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record the fix in CHANGELOG
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the corrected rule recorded in the changelog per context/rules/git.md.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md has a '### Fixed' subsection under '## [Unreleased]' (created if absent, ordered per Keep a Changelog)
It has a one-line imperative bullet describing the corrected context/rules/memory.md daily-log persistence claim and the new guard probe, linking issue #101
Typecheck passes
